---
title: "ACCEPT at VUW"
body-classes: home
---

::: {.hero}
<h1 class="hero-title">ACCEPT at VUW</h1>
<p class="hero-subtitle">Applied causal and cultural evidence for policy translation</p>
:::

ACCEPT at VUW is a research group in the School of Psychological Sciences at Te Herenga Waka - Victoria University of Wellington.

## Why ACCEPT at VUW

Does participation in an after-school tutoring programme *cause* better marks? Simple observation might show participants have higher scores, but these students might also be more motivated or have more supportive parents. Distinguishing the programme's true effect from background factors is the central challenge of evaluation. ACCEPT at VUW (**A**pplied **C**ausal and **C**ross-Cultural **E**vidence for **P**olicy **T**ranslation) focuses on that challenge, using causal inference and machine learning to estimate *whether* interventions work, *for whom* they work best, and *when* they provide benefit. We address open questions in psychological science, culture, and public policy. ACCEPT at VUW is based in the School of Psychological Sciences at Te Herenga Waka - Victoria University of Wellington, New Zealand.

## What We Do

We study causal questions in the social sciences using observational and experimental data.

We teach practical causal methods through coursework, workshops, and supervision.

We collaborate with researchers and communities to produce evidence for policy translation.

## What We Study

### Causal Methods in Social Science

We state clear causal questions, then build doubly robust models to answer them. We use causal forests to estimate heterogeneous treatment effects, and we study longitudinal modified treatment policies for multi-exposure settings.

### Application Domains

We study volunteering and charity, institutional trust, political psychology, personality, health and well-being, meaning and purpose, spirituality, and religion.

## Recent Work

Visit our [Presentations](presentations.qmd) section to explore recent research talks and workshops.

## Start Here

Visit our [about](about.qmd) section for tools and materials to get started.

## Collaborate

If you would like to collaborate or discuss opportunities, email us directly or visit the [about](about.qmd) page.

[Email: joseph.bulbulia@vuw.ac.nz](mailto:joseph.bulbulia@vuw.ac.nz){.btn .btn-primary}

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Updates
Stay updated with our latest research, publications, and events in the [news](news.qmd) section.
:::
